name: Grilled Swordfish on Herbed Couscous W/Vegetable Minestrone
recipeIngredient: 2 cups Finely diced vegetables (such as yellow squash, Zucchini, onion, red bell pepper)
Sprigs of fresh thyme or Rosemary, for garnish (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. To make the vegetable minestrone, heat olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat. Add garlic and cook until it starts to sizzle; do not brown.
2. Add herbs, V-8 juice, chicken stock, and white pepper. Bring to a boil. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 3 minutes. Set aside.
3. To make the couscous, combine chicken stock, black pepper, herbs, and olive oil in a small saucepan. Bring to a boil. Add couscous and mix well. Remove from heat. Cover tightly and allow to stand for at least 5 minutes, or until all liquid is absorbed. Set aside.
4. Wash swordfish and pat dry. Rub with olive oil and season with salt, black pepper, and herbs (if desired).
5. Cook over charcoal grill or under broiler until opaque, about 3 to 4 minutes per side. Do not overcook; fish will become tough.
6. Serve by placing a half cup of couscous on each plate. Top with grilled swordfish. Spoon minestrone around each serving. Garnish with fresh thyme or rosemary (if desired).
